summary: Build the production Web simulation Worker runtime foundation
acceptance_criteria: Normal Web startup boots a dedicated production simulation Worker through a versioned SimulationPort transport; the existing hello adapter, fixture snapshot, canary, Storybook, or test harness cannot satisfy production boot.
The Worker owns WorldState and executes sim-core; browser main-thread React, renderer, client-core stores, and Electron surfaces never import or call sim-core state mutation APIs directly.

The production transport supports Boot, Query, Preview, Submit, Read Model subscription, Save, Load, and SetSpeed request families with serializable envelopes, correlation IDs, protocol versioning, explicit errors, and deterministic ordering.
Boot, graceful shutdown, unexpected termination, reconnect, and recovery expose bounded lifecycle states and cannot report success while no authoritative Worker session exists.
A new Worker session invalidates stale request, response, delta, and command-generation data from the prior session; duplicate or late messages cannot mutate or acknowledge the new authority.
Browser and Node contract runners boot the same seed/content inputs to the same initial deterministic state hash, while the in-process adapter remains test-only.
The foundation publishes stable integration seams for later command, read-model, time-control, and save tasks without fixture fallbacks or local-success placeholders.
notes: This is the only child eligible after RR1-TASK-GRAPH-001 is CLOSED on origin/main.
Any protocol addition must remain minimal, schema-validated, serializable, and shared by Web and Electron clients.
No product implementation starts from DRAFT; Lead must transition and route this task after graph integration.
next_action: Lead is locally completing RR1-WEB-WORKER-RUNTIME-001 after three client_engineer writer shutdowns; route completed work to systems_architect and qa_reviewer before acceptance.
lead_implementation_takeover_reason: Three client_engineer writers closed without terminal handoff; only inherited allowed protocol partial diff remains.
